jsonschema = """{"fields": [{"metadata": {},"name": "Process_Date","nullable": false,"type": "timestamp"}, {"metadata": {},"name": "Filepath","nullable":false,"type": "string"},{"metadata": {},"name": "File_Modified_Date","nullable": false,"type": "timestamp"}]}"""
db_name =  "odw_config_db"
table_name = "datalabs_log"
target_folder = "config/datalabs_log/"
secret_name="datalab-connectionstring"
kv_linked_service="ls_kv"
share_name ="datalab"
directory_path = "ODW/HR/Weekly"

from notebookutils import mssparkutils

def deep_ls(path: str, max_depth=1):
    """
    List all files and folders in specified path and subfolders within maximum recursion depth.
    """
    li = mssparkutils.fs.ls(path)
    for x in li:
        if x.isDir:
            yield x
            if max_depth > 1:
                for y in deep_ls(x.path, max_depth - 1):
                    yield y
        else:
            yield x

def get_latest_folder(files):
    latest_folder = None
    latest_modify_time = 0
    for file in files:
        if file.isDir and file.modifyTime > latest_modify_time:
            latest_folder = file
            latest_modify_time = file.modifyTime
    return latest_folder

# Define the target container
linked_service_name = 'ls_datalab'
directory_path = 'datalab/ODW/HR/Weekly/'  # Replace with your actual directory path
target_container = "abfss://odw-raw@" + spark.sparkContext.environment.get('dataLakeAccountName', 'get') + ".dfs.core.windows.net/"+directory_path
print(target_container)

# List files and folders recursively
files = list(deep_ls(target_container, max_depth=20))

# Get the latest folder
latest_folder = get_latest_folder(files)

if latest_folder:
    # Extract just the folder name
    folder_name = latest_folder.name.split('/')[-1]
    print(f"Latest folder: {folder_name} - Modified Time: {latest_folder.modifyTime}")
else:
    print("No folders found.")

from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
spark = SparkSession.builder.getOrCreate()
akv_name=spark.sparkContext.environment.get('keyVaultName', 'get')
target_container = "abfss://odw-raw@" + spark.sparkContext.environment.get('dataLakeAccountName', 'get') + ".dfs.core.windows.net/"
print(target_container)

azfileshare_new_files(akv_name=akv_name,secret_name=secret_name, kv_linked_service=kv_linked_service, share_name=share_name, directory_path=directory_path, jsonschema=jsonschema, target_container=target_container,target_folder=target_folder, db_name=db_name, table_name=table_name)
